column1: # Publishes the methodology-strict Tier 1 outputs of the gene_sizes pipeline.
# Tier 1 = species with full UTR-bearing exon annotation (>= 50% genes with
# derivable UTR). 40 species, 7 metrics: Gene_Size, Transcript_Size, CDS_Size,
# Source globs are relative to this manifest directory (workflow-RUN_*).

column6: Tier 1 (all_inclusive) workflow purpose, input format (15-col gene-coordinates TSV), output structure, and how to run.
User-editable workflow configuration (input paths, project name, resume flag). Off by default — flip on for full reproducibility.

Wide-format table: one row per processed species; columns are 5 stats (gene_count median mean min max) for each of 7 metrics (Gene_Size Transcript_Size CDS_Size Protein_Size Exon_Count Intron_Total_Size UTR_Total_Size). The headline cross-species deliverable.

Per-species PROCESSED or SKIPPED_NO_DATA or SKIPPED_INCOMPLETE classification across the GIGANTIC species set, with reason. Documents which species are in the 40-species Tier 1 set and which were dropped (and why).

column1: # ----- Ranked gene metrics: {descriptor} (the underlying granular data) -----

column6: Per-gene metrics with within-genome quantile ranks (15 columns per file). One file per species in Tier 1. Used downstream for cross-species rank-conservation analyses.
Per-species per-metric distribution stats (gene_count mean median min max p25 p75) for the 7 Tier 1 metrics.
